items: Do not allow debris or water to contaminate the brake fluid when topping off.
Do not use different brands of brake fluid to prevent chemical changes.
Genuine Honda Ultra Brake Fluid DOT3 or DOT4 must be used.
Do not allow brake fluid to adhere to painted surfaces. If it does adhere, wash it off with water immediately.
Do not reuse drained brake fluid.

blocks.text: Remove the reservoir tank cap (B) from the reservoir tank (A). Remove the filter from the reservoir tank. Siphon fluid and sediment from the tank.
Install the filter on the reservoir tank. Fill the reservoir to the MAX (upper limit) line (A).
Brake fluid should be bled from the front right side.

Remove the bleed screw cap and connect the transparent tube (A) to the bleed screw to receive brake fluid in a suitable container (B). The assistant slowly depresses the brake pedal several times and holds it down hard. Loosen the bleed screw and allow the air to drain out with the brake fluid. If the brake fluid no longer flows out briskly (brake pedal touches the floor), tighten the bleed screw. Repeat Step 2 through Step 4 until no air comes out of the bleed screw and the bleed screw is tightened. Be careful not to run out of brake fluid in the reservoir tank during operation, and replenish it if necessary. Perform steps 1 through 5 until air is removed from each wheel.
Tighten each bleed screw to the specified torque. Tighten the bleed screw using a flare nut wrench (commercially available tool). Install a bleed screw cap on each bleed screw.
Check the amount of brake fluid in the reservoir tank and add brake fluid as necessary. Test-run in a safe place to check braking performance.
